title: birth rates post 2I
wikitext:
[]: birth rates post-2I             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 3:51 pm Is there any evidence in the series that birth rates had dropped after Second Impact? It's sort of implied in the loneliness of the streets and roads, the quiet of the schools, but the fact of it defies history; every catastrophe with major loss of life has been followed by a baby boom and an exponential population growth the following generation. In Rebuild you have a much more densely civilized Tokyo-3, so I'm trying to figure out if this was a feature of the original series that has been changed in the latest version.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Apr 11, 2008 4:05 pm There's an archived thread you can check out, starting somewhere around here: http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=122057#122057 where it touched on the decline of birthrates, but not pertaining to rebuild. Please be careful to keep rebuild discussion in the rebuild sub-forum since there are still people waiting for the DVD release before seeing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 11, 2008 4:07 pm It's more a reflection of Japanese malaise, the first developed country to reach negative population growth.

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 4:38 pm I suspected that, and found support for it in the thread Ornette pointed out for me, ty Ornette. I believe Anno is replacing statements he was making about society at the time of the series production with statements about current society for Rebuild. I don't think there's anything spoilerish about this, but if this thread needs to be moved, then maybe it can be carried on in the Rebuild subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 11, 2008 5:31 pm I myself have wondered if this declining birthrate was related to the CHamber of Guf.....but what if it is purely psychological in nature? What is this about negative population growth you mention Tines? (and what about Russia?)

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 7:19 pm V wrote: I myself have wondered if this declining birthrate was related to the CHamber of Guf.....but what if it is purely psychological in nature?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 11, 2008 8:11 pm However, this would be congruent with Evas not having inherent souls of their own -- you've got these life-forms, even artificially created, that don't spontaneously generate a soul? This suggests that soul-production is strictly mechanical, and when the generator runs out of gas, the assembly line stops. No more spirituality for the next 200 miles.

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 10:18 pm Creating souls is out of our hands, but if you create life by any means, it takes on a life of it's own. The first documented cloning success wasn't until a year after the series ended and the movie released, but it showed that a cloned sheep wasn't behaviorally any different from a born sheep. I suspect it'd be the same for humans (or Angels/Eva,) but we can't know that until it's done. EDIT: Damn, just saying that has filled me with spec that would only be appropriate to Rebuild discussions.

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Fri Apr 11, 2008 10:31 pm V wrote: one of the central scientific principles within the storyverse is that clones are just meat. Clones are lifeless flesh. If you clone something it has no life to it; you cannot clone a soul to go with it.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 11, 2008 10:39 pm they're not cognizant, yes

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 10:39 pm You mean these particular clones, the Evas and Reis. Real life instances show awareness, response, decision-making abilities and there's no reason not to believe they possess sentience as well (but that's a subjective quality that even studying a human clone won't conclusively prove.) So a soulless clone must've been made that way by design. They either had some organ left out in the case of the Angels, or something disconnected on standby in the case of Rei, in order not have a soul. This would be a smart move on Nerv's part, since the corollary to 'when you create life, it takes on a life of it's own' would be 'you never really know what you're going to get.'

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Fri Apr 11, 2008 11:01 pm V wrote: they're not cognizant, yes

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 11, 2008 11:09 pm ORAMI!? wrote: V wrote: they're not cognizant, yes

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Fri Apr 11, 2008 11:24 pm ORAMI!? wrote: And DokEnkephalin, you are equating the Evaverse and the Real World, which can be a bad idea when debating Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Apr 12, 2008 3:29 am IIRC Birthrate decline was nicked from/was homage to Clarke's Childhood's End which has been huge influence on Evangelion. And since it fitted with Anno's, uhh, statements about society (which are more numerous than one might presume) it ended up in the show. I fail to see story reasons for the fact. V wrote: ORAMI!? wrote: V wrote: they're not cognizant, yes

[]:             Quote Post by ORAMI!? » Sat Apr 12, 2008 4:21 am V wrote: ORAMI!? wrote: I think a clear case can be made that the soulless Rei clones are at least partially cognizant.

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Sat Apr 12, 2008 9:27 am Xard wrote: IIRC Birthrate decline was nicked from/was homage to Clarke's Childhood's End which has been huge influence on Evangelion. And since it fitted with Anno's, uhh, statements about society (which are more numerous than one might presume) it ended up in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 12, 2008 9:44 am ORAMI!? wrote: While I agree that you cannot clone/copy/digitize a soul, the soulless Rei clones in the Reiquarium are clearly both alive and aware

[]:             Quote Post by DokEnkephalin » Sat Apr 12, 2008 10:30 am Reichu wrote: ORAMI!? wrote: While I agree that you cannot clone/copy/digitize a soul, the soulless Rei clones in the Reiquarium are clearly both alive and aware

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 12, 2008 10:45 am DokEnkephalin wrote: This doesn't explain the dummy cores of the harpies that weren't manufactured in Tokyo-3.
